items.q: What type of joint is the knee?
items.why: Students confuse knee with hip. Both are in the leg, but the hip is ball-and-socket (multi-directional); the knee is a hinge (mostly flexion-extension). Ball-and-socket joints have more range.

items.q: Most movements in the human body use which class of lever?
items.why: Students assume the most familiar (first class - seesaw) is most common. Actually most body levers are third class (effort between fulcrum and load) - like the biceps curl. They sacrifice mechanical advantage for speed/range.

items.q: What is the difference between aerobic and muscular endurance?
items.correct: Aerobic endurance is the cardiovascular system's ability to supply oxygen; muscular endurance is a muscle's ability to contract repeatedly without fatigue.
items.misconception: They are different names for the same component.
items.random_wrong: Aerobic is for legs, muscular for arms.
items.why: Students treat both as 'being able to keep going'. Aerobic endurance is WHOLE-BODY cardio capacity; muscular endurance is LOCALISED to specific muscles (e.g., sit-up reps).

items.correct: Sex discrimination in US federally funded education and sport
items.why: It's Sex discrimination in US federally funded education and sport. “Disability access only” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: Allocating resources by need. “Allocating the same to everyone” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If 60% of UK women played sport monthly in 2015 vs 75% of men, gender gap (percentage points) is?
items.why: Exact answer: 15. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
